INHALE 2 PUFFS BY MOUTH EVERY 6 HOURS AS NEEDED FOR WHEEZING OR SHORTNES OF BREATH
omega-3 acid ethyl esters 1 gram capsule
TAKE 2 CAPSULES BY MOUTH 2 TIMES A DAY AS DIRECTED
take 1 tablet by mouth once daily
Place 1 tablet twice a day by translingual route before meal(s) for 2 days, for severe nausea only.
Ozempic 0.25 mg or 0.5 mg (2 mg/3 mL) subcutaneous pen injector
DIAL 0.25 MG AND INJECT UNDER THE SKIN WEEKLY AS DIRECTED
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH DAILY FOR VAGINAL YEAST INFECTION
Take 2 tablets 3 times a day by oral route.
TEST BLOOD SUGAR ONCE A DAY
Take 1 tablet every 4-6 hours by oral route as needed for 10 days, for vertigo.

content.content: Patient is a 65-year-old female with past medical history significant for depression anxiety, dyslipidemia, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus type 2, leukocytosis, bilateral knee pain, asthma that presents to clinic today to follow-up from her last encounter.

Patient with uncontrolled diabetes mellitus type 2 was started on Ozempic unfortunately did not tolerate the medication due to the excessive abdominal pain and nausea vomiting.Patient reports doing much better since last encounter patient mentions that she we will continue with the Januvia and metformin and patient would like to discuss her lab results. Patient reports she has refills on medication. She denies any symptoms of increased thirst, increased hunger, increased appetite chest pain, palpitations or malaise.
Patient also reports that she needs refills on her atorvastatin for cholesterol management denies any side effects on this medication.
Constitutional: General Appearance: healthy-appearing, well-developed, and obese . Level of Distress: no acute distress. Ambulation: ambulates without assistance.
Psychiatric: Insight: good judgement. Mental Status: active and alert and normal mood. Orientation: oriented to person, place, time, time, and place.
Eyes: Lids and Conjunctivae: no discharge or pallor and non-injected. Pupils: PERRLA. EOM: EOMI. Sclerae: non-icteric.
Neck: Neck: supple, FROM, trachea midline, and no masses. Lymph Nodes: no cervical LAD, supraclavicular LAD, axillary LAD, or inguinal LAD. Thyroid: no enlargement or nodules and non-tender.

Lungs: Respiratory effort: no dyspnea. Percussion: no dullness, flatness, or hyperresonance. Auscultation: no wheezing, rales/crackles, or rhonchi and breath sounds normal, good air movement, and CTA except as noted.

Cardiovascular: Apical Impulse: not displaced. Heart Auscultation: normal S1 and S2; no murmurs, rubs, or gallops; and RRR. Neck vessels: no carotid bruits. Pulses including femoral / pedal: normal throughout.
Musculoskeletal:: Motor Strength and Tone: normal tone and motor strength. Joints, Bones, and Muscles: normal movement of all extremities and no bony abnormalities. Extremities: no cyanosis or edema.
Neurologic: Gait and Station: normal gait and station. Coordination and Cerebellum: no tremor.
Skin: Inspection and palpation: no rash, lesions, ulcer, induration, nodules, jaundice, or abnormal nevi and good turgor. Nails: normal.

tr.td: Constitutional: no significant weight change, good appetite, no fever, normal activity level, no fatigue
Eyes: no pain, no blurry vision, no redness, no itchiness, no swelling, no discharge, normal movement
ENMT: no ear pain, no ear discharge, no hearing loss, no sinus pressure, no drooling, no facial swelling, no congestion, no sore throat, no hoarseness, no mouth lesions
Cardiovascular: no chest pain, normal heart rate
Breasts: no lumps, no tenderness, no discharge
Respiratory: no cough, no wheezing, no chest tightness, no pain with respiration, normal respiration
GI: no difficulty swallowing, no abdominal pain, no nausea, no vomiting, no diarrhea, no constipation, no blood in stool, no mucous in stool
Musculoskeletal: no soft tissue swelling, no joint swelling, no myalgia, moves all extremities well, no previous injuries, no trauma
Skin: no pain, no itchiness, no skin dryness, no flaking, no redness, no rash, no hives, no skin lesions, no skin lumps, no swelling, no bruising, no insect bites
Psych: no depression, no anxiety, no insomnia, no stress, no loss of interest

tr.th: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported

translation.displayName: Vital signs, weight, height, head circumference, oximetry, BMI, and BSA panel
Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

code.displayName: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported

td.content: What Is Your Level Of Alcohol Consumption?
Did you used to be able to walk further than you do now?
Has tobacco cessation counseling been provided?
What is your level of caffeine consumption?
Have there been any changes to your family or social situation?
Do you have smoke and carbon monoxide detectors in your home?
Are you passively exposed to smoke?
Are there any guns present in your home?
Do you use insect repellent routinely?
What was the date of your most recent tobacco screening?
What is the highest grade or level of school you have completed or the highest degree you have received?
Which of your hands is dominant?
What type of diet are you following?
Do you have an advance directive?
Is blood transfusion acceptable in an emergency?

text.paragraph: No social history survey screeners recorded
No social history SDOH screeners recorded

td.content: Do you or have you ever used any other forms of tobacco or nicotine?
What is your level of alcohol consumption?
Do you use any illicit or recreational drugs?
Are you able to care for yourself independently?
Are you blind or do you have difficulty seeing?
Are you deaf or do you have serious difficulty hearing?
How often did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past year?
How many drinks containing alcohol did you have on a typical day when you were drinking in the past year?
How often did you have six or more drinks on one occasion in the past year?
Do you have difficulty concentrating, remembering or making decisions?
Do you feel stressed (tense, restless, nervous, or anxious, or unable to sleep at night)?
Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?
Feeling nervous, anxious or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

tr.td:
SAGUARO SCOTTSDALE CAMELBACK RD
100 N Central Ave.,#300 SCOTTSDALE, CA 85251-4500

td.content: Patient with a chronic history of diabetes mellitus type 2 uncontrolled patient has been noncompliant with diet and management. Patient reports since last encounter she is make changes and her fasting glucose levels are under 200. Patient reports that they are averaging in the 150s. Patient is hopeful that her metformin Januvia and her diet will help improve her numbers she is aware that in September her A1c was 12.3%. Patient was here today to follow-up from her Ozempic unfortunately she did not tolerate the medication due to severe nausea. Patient will continue with just the Januvia metformin and diet. Will be pending her next A1c. Patient denies any ongoing symptoms of nausea vomiting or abdominal symptoms post Ozempic. Patient reports having normal bowel movements.

Patient was counseled in details about the importance of following healthy diet low in carbohydrates and sugary products. The patient was encouraged to reduce the amount of rice, bread, potatoes, tortilla and chips from their diet as well as replacing sugary drinks and soda with beverages reduced in sugar, preferably water. Hydration importance discussed.

Also stressed importance of exercise minimum 45 minutes a day 3-4 times a week. Stressed the importance of compliance to medications and lifestyle modification to avoids complications of DMT2. Also recommended glucose levels to be taken at home and written into logs to be brought into clinic at next encounter.
Last foot exam (visual inspection, sensory exam with monofilament, and pulse check): Pending next encounter
Last retinal eye exam: Patient reports an upcoming appointment needs referral
Patient is on an ACE: yes
Patient is on a Statin: yes
The patient is aware that annual foot and eye exam are necessary.

tr.td:
SAGUARO SCOTTSDALE CAMELBACK RD
100 N Central Ave.,#300 SCOTTSDALE, CA 85251-4500

td.content: Patient with a chronic history of diabetes mellitus type 2 uncontrolled previous non-compliant with diet and DM management, now doing better. Prevoius A1C was 12% now 8.4%.
Patient now on januvia 100 mg, lisinopril 2.5 mg, metformin 1000 mg
Patient doing well, she will continue with oral medication and diet.
Patient reports she will increase her walks

Patient was counseled in details about the importance of following healthy diet low in carbohydrates and sugary products. The patient was encouraged to reduce the amount of rice, bread, potatoes, tortilla and chips from their diet as well as replacing sugary drinks and soda with beverages reduced in sugar, preferably water. Hydration importance discussed.

Also stressed importance of exercise minimum 45 minutes a day 3-4 times a week. Stressed the importance of compliance to medications and lifestyle modification to avoids complications of DMT2. Also recommended glucose levels to be taken at home and written into logs to be brought into clinic at next encounter.
Last foot exam (visual inspection, sensory exam with monofilament, and pulse check): Pending next encounter
Last retinal eye exam: Patient reports an upcoming appointment needs referral
Patient is on an ACE: yes
Patient is on a Statin: yes
The patient is aware that annual foot and eye exam are necessary.
Patient with history of dyslipidemia needs refills on atorvastatin. Patient is on statin reports doing well on the medication.
Patient with BMI of 39 indicating obese
Patient aware of health risks associated with obesity
Diet, exercise, and lifestyle changes advised.
Patient with chronic vertigo appears to be BPPV (+ DHP maneuver)
Discussed with patient that based on history and physical exam it appears that patient is suffering from benign paroxysmal positional vertigo bilaterally recurrent
Discussed with patient etiology and management in addition to prognosis.
All questions and concerns answered today.
Patient will be given meclizine and Zofran to be used as needed.

Epley maneuver to be done at home was discussed with patient as well we did go over a YouTube video so patient is able to understand the maneuver. In addition to sending a stat referral to vestibular therapy.
Red flags discussed with patient if they were to occur patient is to follow-up in clinic for further recommendations.

Patient noted to have leukocytosis on recent labs she is aware that we would like to repeat that she is unsure if perhaps she had a cold when she did her lab work. New labs ordered today. Further recommendations pending results.
Note: 02/18/2026 assess encounter patient continues to have mild leukocytosis less than 12,000. Will monitor for now repeat advised. Orders placed today. Will follow-up with patient next encounter.

td.content: Patient is a 65-year-old female with past medical history significant for depression anxiety, dyslipidemia, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus type 2, leukocytosis, bilateral knee pain, asthma that presents to clinic today to follow-up from her last encounter.

Patient with uncontrolled diabetes mellitus type 2 was started on Ozempic unfortunately did not tolerate the medication due to the excessive abdominal pain and nausea vomiting.Patient reports doing much better since last encounter patient mentions that she we will continue with the Januvia and metformin and patient would like to discuss her lab results. Patient reports she has refills on medication. She denies any symptoms of increased thirst, increased hunger, increased appetite chest pain, palpitations or malaise.
Patient also reports that she needs refills on her atorvastatin for cholesterol management denies any side effects on this medication.

Elena Chen, MD
700 E Indian School Rd,STE 300, Gilbert, CA, 85233-1200, US
